---
## Goal
Use when testing local web applications with Playwright — verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing screenshots, viewing browser logs.


## Description

Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

## When to Use

- Testing local web applications
- Verifying frontend functionality
- Debugging UI behavior
- Capturing screenshots for documentation
- Viewing browser console logs
- Automated UI testing
- End-to-end testing

## When NOT to Use

- Production testing (use production tools)
- Non-web applications
- Real-time collaborative testing
- Performance testing

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Setup Playwright

- Install Playwright
- Configure browser settings
- Set up test environment
- Prepare test data

### Phase 2: Create Tests

- Write test scenarios
- Identify UI elements
- Plan test flow
- Add assertions

### Phase 3: Execute Tests

- Run tests
- Capture screenshots
- View browser logs
- Debug failures

### Phase 4: Analyze & Report

- Review test results
- Document findings
- Fix issues
- Archive test results

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: systematic-debugging, verification-before-completion
- **Playwright**: https://playwright.dev
- **Browsers**: Chromium, Firefox, WebKit
- **Debugging**: Inspector, trace viewer

## Best Practices

- Write clear test scenarios
- Use meaningful selectors
- Capture screenshots for documentation
- Check browser logs for errors
- Test responsive design
- Test cross-browser compatibility
- Maintain test suite
